fields.name: Kakum National Park
fields.description: Famous canopy walkway in Ghana.

fields.name: Wli WaterFalls
fields.description: A nice place to swim

fields.name: Labadi Beach (La Pleasure Beach)
fields.description: Accra's most popular and vibrant beach, stretching over two kilometres of golden Atlantic coastline. Known for its lively atmosphere, live highlife and afrobeats music on weekends, horse rides, beach volleyball, and a thriving food-and-drink scene. One of the best places to experience everyday Ghanaian beach culture at its most energetic.

fields.name: Kwame Nkrumah Mausoleum
fields.description: The final resting place of Ghana's founding father and Africa's greatest pan-Africanist, Dr. Kwame Nkrumah. The memorial park features a striking fountain, a museum chronicling Ghana's independence struggle, and a reflective garden. A must-visit for anyone seeking to understand modern Ghana's remarkable journey from colonialism to freedom on 6 March 1957.
fields.location: High Street, Accra Central, Greater Accra Region

fields.name: Accra Arts Centre (Centre for National Culture)
fields.description: A sprawling open-air market and cultural hub in the heart of Accra, housing hundreds of stalls selling woodcarvings, kente fabric, beads, batik, bronze figurines, and traditional Ghanaian artefacts. The Centre also hosts live drumming and dance performances, making it both the best shopping destination and a living showcase of Ghanaian artisanship.
fields.location: Barnes Road, Accra, Greater Accra Region

fields.name: Makola Market
fields.description: One of West Africa's busiest and most famous open-air markets, Makola is the commercial heartbeat of Accra. Vendors sell everything from fresh produce, textiles, electronics and household goods to traditional medicine and street food. Wandering its labyrinthine alleys is an exhilarating cultural immersion into the everyday rhythms of Ghanaian city life.
fields.location: Makola, Accra Central, Greater Accra Region

fields.name: Independence Arch & Black Star Square
fields.description: Ghana's most iconic national monument, the Independence Arch stands tall at the entrance to Black Star Square — the largest public square in West Africa. Built to commemorate Ghana's independence in 1957, the square has hosted presidential inaugurations, military parades, and national celebrations. The inscribed words 'Freedom and Justice — AD 1957' remain one of Africa's most powerful statements of self-determination.

fields.name: Cape Coast Castle
fields.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the most emotionally profound historical monuments in the world. Built by Swedish traders in 1653 and later used by the British as the headquarters of the transatlantic slave trade, the castle's dungeons held tens of thousands of enslaved Africans before they passed through the 'Door of No Return' onto waiting ships. Today it stands as a memorial to those who suffered, a testament to resilience, and a cornerstone of Ghana's heritage tourism.

fields.name: Elmina Castle (São Jorge da Mina)
fields.description: The oldest European building in sub-Saharan Africa, constructed by the Portuguese in 1482 and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Elmina Castle served successive colonial powers — Portuguese, Dutch, and British — and was the largest slave-holding site on the West African coast. Its whitewashed walls and ocean setting belie the harrowing history within; a guided tour through its dungeons, female slave quarters, and governor's chamber is a deeply moving and essential experience.

fields.name: Assin Manso Slave River
fields.description: A deeply significant heritage site marking the last point where enslaved Africans were allowed to bathe and drink water before the forced march to the coast and the waiting slave ships. The site includes the 'Ancestral Return Cemetery', where the remains of two enslaved Africans — one from Jamaica and one from the United States — were repatriated and interred in 1998. A place of profound reflection and reconciliation.

fields.name: Hans Cottage Botel
fields.description: A uniquely Ghanaian eco-tourism experience — a lakeside lodge built on stilts above a lagoon teeming with live crocodiles. Visitors can dine on a terrace directly above the water and observe the crocodiles swimming beneath their feet, or walk out onto platforms to watch them bask in the sun. The resident crocodiles are considered sacred by the local community, making this a fascinating meeting point of wildlife and living tradition.

fields.name: Manhyia Palace Museum
fields.description: The official residence of the Asantehene — the King of the Ashanti — and one of Ghana's most important cultural landmarks. The museum in the original 1925 palace building traces the history of the Ashanti Kingdom through royal artefacts, photographs, regalia, and detailed accounts of the Anglo-Ashanti Wars. An audience with the Asantehene's court on special occasions is among the most memorable experiences in Ghana.

fields.name: Kejetia Market (Kumasi Central Market)
fields.description: One of the largest markets in West Africa, covering over 12 hectares and housing an estimated 10,000 stalls. The market is the trading nerve centre of Ghana, selling everything from kente cloth, spices, and live animals to second-hand goods and traditional medicines. Shopping for fresh kente fabric directly from Ashanti weavers here is an experience unavailable anywhere else.

fields.name: Bonwire Kente Weaving Village
fields.description: The spiritual home of kente — Ghana's most celebrated and internationally recognised handwoven textile. Bonwire weavers have been producing kente on narrow-strip looms for over 400 years, with each pattern carrying specific Akan proverbs and meanings. Visitors can watch master weavers at work, learn about the symbolism of different patterns and colours, and purchase authentic, handmade kente cloth directly from the artisans.
fields.location: Bonwire, Kwabre East District, Ashanti Region

fields.name: Lake Bosomtwe
fields.description: Ghana's only natural lake, formed approximately 1.07 million years ago by a meteorite impact in the Ashanti forest. The lake is considered sacred by the Ashanti people — who believe the souls of the dead come here to bid farewell to the god Twi — and traditional fishermen still paddle on it using wooden planks rather than boats. Surrounded by lush hills and small fishing villages, it offers swimming, hiking, and a profound sense of peace.

fields.name: Kumasi Fort & Military Museum
fields.description: Built by the British in 1897 following the Third Anglo-Ashanti War, Kumasi Fort is the last surviving British military fort in Ghana. It now houses the Ghana Armed Forces Museum, with exhibits covering Ghana's military history from pre-colonial Ashanti warfare through both World Wars to present-day peacekeeping operations. The fort sits within a well-maintained compound in the centre of Kumasi city.

fields.name: Wli Waterfalls
fields.description: The tallest waterfall in West Africa, cascading 80 metres in two tiers into a cool, mist-filled basin surrounded by the lush Agumatsa Wildlife Sanctuary. The lower falls are accessible via a scenic 45-minute hike through butterfly-filled forest paths and a colony of over 300,000 straw-coloured fruit bats. The upper falls require a more demanding 3–4 hour trek but reward hikers with breathtaking views across the forest canopy to Togo.

fields.name: Tafi Atome Monkey Sanctuary
fields.description: A community-managed sacred forest sanctuary that is home to a large troop of Mona monkeys, considered sacred by the local Ewe community and protected by a centuries-old taboo against harming them. Visitors can walk through the forest with a local guide and hand-feed the habituated monkeys, which climb onto arms and shoulders freely. One of Ghana's most delightful and intimate wildlife encounters.
fields.location: Tafi Atome, Hohoe District, Volta Region

fields.name: Kpando Torkor (Lake Volta Crossing)
fields.description: Lake Volta — the world's largest man-made lake by surface area — is best experienced from the town of Kpando, where canoe trips across the glassy water reveal a haunting landscape of half-submerged trees, remote fishing villages, and spectacular sunsets. The lake was created in 1965 by the Akosombo Dam and its vast, mirror-like surface stretches 400 km northward through the heart of Ghana.

fields.name: Mount Afadjato
fields.description: Tallest Mountain in West Africa
fields.location: Liati Wote, Hohoe District, Volta Region

fields.name: Ada Estuary & Foothills
fields.description: Where the Volta River meets the Atlantic Ocean at one of Ghana's most spectacular and tranquil beach destinations. Ada Foah offers a rare combination of river, ocean, and estuary in one sweeping landscape. Activities include canoe and motorboat trips through the estuary mangroves, sandbar walks, kite-surfing on the river, and relaxing at eco-lodges set along the golden riverbanks. The turtles that nest here between October and January add another remarkable dimension to any visit.
fields.location: Ada Foah, Greater Accra / Volta Region border

fields.name: Mole National Park
fields.description: Ghana's largest and most important wildlife refuge, protecting 4,840 km² of Guinea savanna woodland and its extraordinary biodiversity. Mole is home to over 90 mammal species including forest elephants, buffalo, hippopotamus, warthog, kob, roan antelope, waterbuck, and green and colobus monkeys, as well as over 300 bird species. The park is famous for its walking safaris — guided walks that bring visitors within metres of wild elephants — an experience that is genuinely world-class and almost unique in Africa.
fields.location: Mole, West Gonja District, Savannah Region

fields.name: Larabanga Mosque
fields.description: One of the oldest mosques in West Africa and Ghana's most significant Islamic heritage site, believed to have been built in 1421 in the distinctive Sudano-Sahelian architectural style — characterised by its earthen construction, protruding wooden beams, and conical minarets. Located at the entrance to Mole National Park, the mosque is considered so sacred that the local community has for centuries refused all attempts at permanent structural alteration, insisting instead on annual replastering with earth.
fields.location: Larabanga, West Gonja District, Savannah Region

fields.name: Tongo Hills & Tengzug Shrine
fields.description: A dramatic landscape of ancient granite outcrops, boulders, and sacred groves in the Upper East Region, home to the Talensi people and their centuries-old animist shrine tradition. The Tengzug Shrine — carved into the base of a massive boulder — is one of the most important and actively used traditional shrines in Ghana, visited by supplicants from across West Africa. The hike through the Tongo Hills to the shrine is itself remarkable for its surreal geological landscape.
fields.location: Tongo, Talensi District, Upper East Region

fields.name: Kintampo Waterfalls
fields.description: A powerful and beautiful waterfall on the Pumpum River in the geographic centre of Ghana. The falls cascade 24 metres down a broad rock face into a clear pool, surrounded by lush forest. A well-maintained path and wooden platforms allow visitors to view the falls from multiple angles. The site also includes natural cave formations in the rock, locally known as the 'Fuller Falls', named after a former colonial administrator.

fields.name: Aburi Botanical Gardens
fields.description: Established in 1890 in the cool highlands of the Akuapem Ridge, 30 km north of Accra, the Aburi Botanical Gardens is one of West Africa's oldest and most beautiful colonial-era gardens. Set at an elevation of 450 metres, the gardens offer a refreshing escape from Accra's heat, with avenues of towering silk cotton trees, tropical plants, a fernery, and a charming canopy walkway. A beloved weekend retreat for Accra residents for over a century.
fields.location: Aburi, Akuapem North District, Eastern Region

fields.name: Boti Falls
fields.description: Twin waterfalls — a male and a female fall — plunging side by side into a shared pool in a beautiful forest reserve in the Eastern Region. According to local Krobo tradition, the two falls join at their base during the rainy season as a symbol of marriage. The surrounding forest is rich with butterflies, birds, and an umbrella rock formation that shelters several hundred people. A short hike from the car park makes this a very accessible yet rewarding natural attraction.
fields.location: Boti, Yilo Krobo District, Eastern Region

fields.name: Akosombo Dam
fields.description: One of Africa's great engineering achievements, the Akosombo Dam was completed in 1965 under President Kwame Nkrumah and created Lake Volta — the world's largest artificial lake by surface area at 8,502 km². The dam still generates a substantial portion of Ghana's electricity. Guided tours of the dam structure offer fascinating insight into this feat of mid-century African infrastructure, with spectacular views of the Volta River gorge and the lake stretching north to the horizon.

fields.name: Fort Metal Cross (Dixcove Fort)
fields.description: A well-preserved British fort perched dramatically on a promontory above the fishing village of Dixcove, offering stunning views along the Western Region coastline. Built in 1691, the fort is far less visited than Cape Coast or Elmina but arguably more atmospheric — its crumbling battlements, cannon emplacements, and intimate scale make it feel genuinely ancient. The village of Dixcove below is one of the most photogenic and authentic fishing communities on the entire Ghanaian coast.
fields.location: Dixcove, Ahanta West District, Western Region

fields.name: Ankasa Conservation Area
fields.description: Ghana's only tract of virgin, undisturbed lowland rainforest — a 509 km² conservation area of extraordinary biodiversity on the border with Côte d'Ivoire. Ankasa protects Ghana's only remaining population of pygmy hippos, as well as forest elephants, leopards, bongo, Diana monkeys, chimpanzees, and over 600 butterfly species. The forest receives very few visitors, making it one of West Africa's true wilderness experiences for serious nature travellers.

fields.title: Afrochella (Afro Future)
fields.description: Held every December during Ghana's 'Detty December' season, Afrochella (rebranded as Afro Future) is a massive Afrobeats and culture festival that attracts thousands of visitors from the African diaspora. The event features headline performances from top Afrobeats, amapiano, and highlife artists, alongside a curated marketplace showcasing African fashion designers, visual artists, food vendors, and lifestyle brands. It has quickly become the centrepiece of December nightlife in Accra.
fields.location: El-Wak Sports Stadium, Accra, Greater Accra Region
highlights: Live performances by top Afrobeats artists
Shuttle service from Accra city centre

fields.title: Hogbetsotso Festival
fields.description: The Hogbetsotso ('Festival of the Exodus') is the grandest festival of the Anlo Ewe people, celebrated on the first Saturday of November in Anloga, the traditional capital of the Anlo state. It commemorates the escape of the Ewe people from the tyrannical King Agorkorli of Notsie (in present-day Togo). Highlights include the spectacular Borborbor and Agbadza dances, a durbar of chiefs in full regalia, libation pouring to ancestors, and the dramatic re-enactment of the legendary exodus. It is one of Ghana's most visually stunning and culturally significant celebrations.
highlights: Transport to Anloga from Accra or Ho
Durbar of chiefs in full regalia

fields.title: Kundum Festival
fields.description: The Kundum festival is celebrated by the Nzema and Ahanta people of the Western Region to mark the harvest season and to drive away evil spirits. Running for several weeks, it features elaborate masquerade dances, communal feasting, drumming, and purification rituals. The festival is one of the oldest harvest celebrations in Ghana and a spectacular display of Western Region culture and traditions.

fields.title: Kente Festival
fields.description: A vibrant celebration of Ghana's world-famous hand-woven Kente cloth in Bonwire, the ancestral home of Kente weaving. Watch master weavers at work, participate in weaving workshops, enjoy traditional Ashanti drumming and dancing, and take home an authentic piece of Ghanaian heritage. The festival draws weavers, designers, and textile enthusiasts from around the globe.
highlights: Authentic Kente strip to take home

fields.title: Executive Leadership Summit — Cape Coast
fields.description: A two-day leadership summit for C-suite executives and senior managers. Held at the historic Cape Coast Castle precinct, combining strategic workshops with heritage tours. Includes keynote speakers, breakout sessions, and a sunset networking dinner overlooking the Atlantic.
fields.suitable_for: C-suite executives, senior managers, corporate groups

fields.title: Corporate Team Retreat — Aburi Gardens
fields.description: Escape the city noise for a full-day corporate team-building retreat at the lush Aburi Botanical Gardens. Activities include facilitated team challenges, a guided botanical walk, and a farm-to-table lunch sourced from the Akuapem Ridge.

fields.title: Family Fun Day — Labadi Beach
fields.description: A full day of sun, sand, and family bonding at Labadi Beach. Enjoy beach games, horseback riding along the shore, live highlife music, and a BBQ cookout as the sun sets over the Gulf of Guinea. Perfect for families with kids of all ages.

fields.title: Friends Reunion Picnic — Lake Bosomtwe
fields.description: Reconnect with old friends at Ghana's only natural lake. Paddle canoes across the crater lake, share stories over a lakeside picnic, and take a gentle hike through the surrounding Ashanti forest. A laid-back day to strengthen bonds.

fields.title: Wellness & Mindfulness Retreat — Wli Waterfalls
fields.description: A three-day wellness retreat at the base of the tallest waterfall in West Africa. Start mornings with yoga by the falls, enjoy guided meditation in the forest, and restore your energy with organic meals prepared by local chefs. Limited to 20 guests for an intimate experience.

fields.title: Creative Writers' Retreat — Kpando Torkor
fields.description: Find your creative voice on the banks of Lake Volta. This four-day retreat pairs daily writing workshops with canoe rides, village storytelling sessions, and quiet lakeside reflection time. End each evening with a bonfire reading circle.

fields.title: Canopy Walkway Adventure — Kakum
fields.description: Get your adrenaline pumping on the famous Kakum Canopy Walkway — 40 metres above the rainforest floor. This full-day adventure includes a guided forest trek, the canopy walk, bird-watching, and a packed bush lunch. Ideal for thrill-seekers and nature lovers.
fields.suitable_for: Adventure seekers, nature lovers, fitness enthusiasts

fields.title: Mountain Hiking Challenge — Mount Afadjato
fields.description: Conquer the highest point in Ghana! This guided hike to the summit of Mount Afadjato (885 m) takes you through waterfalls, butterfly trails, and misty highland scenery. Celebrate at the top with a summit breakfast and panoramic views across the Volta Region.

fields.title: Surprise Anniversary Dinner — Aqua Safari
fields.description: A bespoke anniversary celebration on the Ada Estuary. Arrive by decorated boat, enjoy a private candlelit dinner on a sandbar island, serenaded by a live acoustic duo. Every detail — from the menu to the décor — is tailored to the couple's story.

fields.title: Themed Birthday Safari — Mole National Park
fields.description: Celebrate a milestone birthday in the wild! This two-day custom safari package at Mole National Park includes a jeep game drive, a walking safari with rangers, a bush-themed birthday dinner under the stars, and a sunrise elephant-watching experience.

fields.title: Romantic Sunset Cruise on the Volta
fields.description: Set sail on the majestic Volta River as the sun paints the sky in shades of gold and amber. This intimate couples cruise features a private table for two, live acoustic music, a three-course Ghanaian-fusion dinner, and champagne. Float past lush riverbanks and fishing villages as you create unforgettable memories with your special someone. Perfect for anniversaries, proposals, or simply celebrating love.
highlights: Private table for two on the upper deck

fields.title: Couples Spa & Cultural Retreat at Aburi Gardens
fields.description: Escape the city and reconnect with your partner at the serene Aburi Botanical Gardens. This full-day couples retreat begins with a guided garden walk through tropical plants and scenic hilltop views, followed by a traditional Ghanaian couples spa treatment using shea butter, black soap, and herbal infusions. Enjoy a private lunch in the gardens, a beadmaking workshop for two, and leave with handmade matching bracelets. A truly romantic and culturally enriching experience.

fields.title: Cape Coast & Elmina Heritage Trail
fields.description: Embark on a powerful two-day journey through Ghana's most significant historical landmarks along the Cape Coast. Visit the haunting Cape Coast Castle — a UNESCO World Heritage Site — and walk through the 'Door of No Return' where millions of enslaved Africans were shipped across the Atlantic. Continue to the Elmina Castle, the oldest European building in sub-Saharan Africa, and explore the vibrant Elmina fishing harbour. The tour also includes a serene canopy walk at Kakum National Park, rising 30 metres above the rainforest floor, and a visit to the Hans Cottage Botel — a unique lakeside lodge surrounded by crocodiles. End each day with authentic Fante cuisine and storytelling by local guides.
highlights: Guided tour of Cape Coast Castle (UNESCO World Heritage Site)

fields.title: Accra City & Culture Experience
fields.description: Discover the vibrant soul of Ghana's capital in this immersive full-day tour through Accra's most iconic neighbourhoods and cultural landmarks. Start at the W.E.B. Du Bois Memorial Centre and Kwame Nkrumah Memorial Park — resting place of Ghana's founding father. Dive into the buzzing Makola Market, one of West Africa's largest open-air markets, then head to the creative district of Osu for art galleries, street food, and boutique shopping. The afternoon takes you to the Jamestown lighthouse and the historic fishing community of Ga Mashie, where you'll witness traditional boxing culture and taste fresh kenkey by the shore. The tour wraps up with a sunset dinner at a rooftop restaurant overlooking the city skyline.
highlights: Jamestown lighthouse & Ga Mashie fishing village
Rooftop sunset dinner with city views

itinerary.title: Full-Day Accra Immersion
itinerary.description: 8:00 AM — Hotel pick-up. Visit Kwame Nkrumah Memorial Park and the W.E.B. Du Bois Centre. 10:30 AM — Guided walk through Makola Market. 12:30 PM — Street food tasting in Osu, followed by art gallery visits. 2:30 PM — Drive to Jamestown, climb the lighthouse for panoramic views, and explore Ga Mashie. 5:00 PM — Rooftop dinner with sunset views. 7:00 PM — Drop-off at hotel.

fields.title: Luxury Penthouse at Cantonments
fields.description: A stunning 3-bedroom penthouse with panoramic views of Accra. Features modern furnishings, a fully equipped kitchen, spacious living area with floor-to-ceiling windows, and a private terrace perfect for evening sunsets. Located in the heart of Cantonments, minutes from embassies, restaurants, and shopping.

fields.title: Cozy Beach Villa in Ada Foah
fields.description: A charming 2-bedroom beach villa right on the shores of the Volta River estuary. Wake up to the sound of waves and enjoy direct beach access. The villa features a rustic-modern design with a private outdoor deck, barbecue area, and hammocks under the palms. Ideal for weekend getaways and small group retreats.

fields.name: Toyota Land Cruiser V8 2023
fields.description: A powerful and luxurious Toyota Land Cruiser V8, perfect for long-distance travel, group tours, and off-road adventures across Ghana. Features premium leather interior, advanced climate control, and excellent ground clearance for any terrain. Comes with or without an experienced driver.

fields.name: Mercedes-Benz Sprinter 15-Seater
fields.description: A spacious and comfortable Mercedes-Benz Sprinter van, ideal for group tours, corporate shuttles, airport transfers, and family excursions. Features reclining seats, ample luggage space, and a professional driver option. Air-conditioned throughout for maximum comfort on long drives.

fields.title: Clean Water for Tamale
fields.description: We partnered with local NGOs to install 5 borehole water systems in underserved communities around Tamale, providing clean drinking water to over 2,000 residents. The project included training local technicians for ongoing maintenance.
fields.impact_summary: 5 boreholes installed, providing clean water to 2,000+ residents across 3 villages.

fields.title: School Supplies for Cape Coast
fields.description: Our annual Back-to-School drive collected and distributed school supplies, uniforms, and books to 500 children in Cape Coast. Partnered with 3 local schools to identify students most in need.
fields.impact_summary: 500 students received school supplies and uniforms for the new academic year.

fields.title: Beach Cleanup at Kokrobite
fields.description: Organized a massive community beach cleanup event at Kokrobite Beach, bringing together 150 volunteers to remove over 2 tonnes of plastic waste. The initiative also included workshops on sustainable fishing practices.
fields.impact_summary: 2+ tonnes of waste removed, 150 volunteers engaged in environmental education.

fields.title: Unforgettable Experience!
fields.comment: The Cape Coast tour was absolutely amazing. Our guide was knowledgeable and passionate. The entire trip was well-organized and exceeded our expectations. Highly recommend!

fields.title: Perfect Family Holiday
fields.comment: We booked accommodations through this platform and everything was seamless. The apartment was clean, modern, and in a great location. Will definitely book again!

fields.title: Great Cultural Event
fields.comment: Attended a cultural festival organized through this platform. The performances were beautiful and the food was delicious. Only minor issue was parking, but overall a wonderful experience.

fields.title: Reliable Car Rental
fields.comment: Rented an SUV for a week-long trip through the Volta Region. The vehicle was in excellent condition and pickup/return was hassle-free. Great value for money.

fields.title: A Hidden Gem of a Platform
fields.comment: Found this platform while planning a trip to Ghana and it made everything so easy. From booking tours to finding events, its a one-stop shop for exploring Ghana.

fields.title: Nice Company
fields.comment: Took me on an exciting tour and it was very fine

fields.about_text: At 1957 The Ghana Experience LBG, our mission is to deliver authentic and unforgettable travel experiences that showcase the rich culture, heritage, and natural beauty of Ghana.

fields.title: Custom tour request from Jamin Opoku
